channel.title: Artificial Intelligence on CEDIS / UnB
channel.description: Recent content in Artificial Intelligence on CEDIS / UnB

item.title: Products
item.description: Digital platform by CEDIS/UnB for planning, screening, extraction, and reporting of systematic literature reviews with traceability, collaboration, and reproducible outputs.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science that seeks to develop systems capable of mimicking and enhancing human abilities such as reasoning, learning, and perception. One of its most fascinating subareas is Natural Language Processing (NLP).

item.title: Evidentia Review Project
item.description: Ongoing CEDIS project focused on the continuous research, evolution, and validation of methods, workflows, and capabilities for systematic reviews, with Evidentia Review as its resulting product.

item.title: Products
item.description: An AI API for academic performance prediction in gamified environments, complementing the EduTrack platform, with academic support from CEDIS.

item.title: Project DFCris
item.description: A research project in partnership with IBICT and funded by FAP/DF.

item.title: People
item.description: Full Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product proposing a chatbot as a support tool for Requirements Engineering.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product for centralizing administrative information to support student development at FCTE, using a chatbot and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product applying machine learning to non-invasive prediction of euploidy in human embryos based on morphokinetic data.

item.title: People
item.description: Works on the development of Evidentia Review, a CEDIS software platform for systematic literature reviews.

item.title: CEDIS presents Evidentia Review for systematic literature reviews
item.description: Evidentia Review is a CEDIS software platform designed for end-to-end systematic literature reviews.

item.title: Contextus software registration is granted by INPI
item.description: The Contextus certificate was issued on February 18, 2026, formalizing the software's legal protection before INPI.

item.title: Three Filters: an undergraduate thesis filters scientific metadata in three layers
item.description: Leonardo Milomes Vitoriano and Mateus de Almeida Dias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis presenting Three Filters — a system to ensure quality and integrity of scientific metadata in JSON.

item.title: A chatbot for FCTE: an undergraduate thesis centralizes administrative information for students
item.description: Breno Henrique de Souza and Giulia Domingues de Alcantara defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis centralizing FCTE administrative information into an AI-supported chatbot.

item.title: Algorithmic bias: an undergraduate thesis systematically maps the ethical challenges
item.description: Amanda Jeniffer Pereira Nobre and Ana Carolina Rodrigues Leite defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis running a systematic literature mapping on the ethical challenges tied to bias in AI algorithms.

item.title: Predicting euploidy without touching the embryo: an undergraduate thesis brings machine learning to IVF
item.description: Maria Eduarda dos Santos Abritta Ferreira and Sabrina Caldas Berno defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying machine learning to the non-invasive prediction of euploidy in human embryos, based on morphokinetic data.

item.title: Chat gpt 4o in requirements elicitation an undergraduate thesis runs the case study the community needed
item.description: Geraldo Victor Alves Barbosa and João Victor de Oliveira Matos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating the use of ChatGPT-4o in the requirements elicitation, discovery, and specification process.

item.title: Article awarded Best Paper at ITHET 2024
item.description: Paper presented at IEEE ITHET 2024 in Paris receives Best Paper award.

item.title: AI in evaluating software courses: a pilot undergraduate thesis tests support for teachers
item.description: Caio César Oliveira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing artificial intelligence to improve individual evaluations in Software Engineering courses.

item.title: AI model for Libras: an undergraduate thesis crosses the communication barrier with the deaf community
item.description: Daniela Soares de Oliveira and Danilo Domingo Vitoriano Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis building an AI model for Brazilian Sign Language translation — a technical response to a communication barrier affecting more than 2.3 million Brazilians.

item.title: Llms for legal labor consultation an undergraduate thesis presents a multimodal chatbot
item.description: Álvaro Henrique de Sousa Gouvea and Luís Guilherme Gaboardi Lins defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis using Large Language Models to build a multimodal chatbot for legal-labor consultation.

item.title: GCMP: a dissertation systematizes how to build gamified chatbots
item.description: Arthur Rocha Temporim de Lacerda defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation proposing GCMP — the Gamified Chatbot Management Process — a method to orchestrate the end-to-end development of gamified chatbots.

item.title: Latte: a duo trains a gamified chatbot to help write scientific articles
item.description: Luiz Henrique Fernandes Zamprogno and Victor Eduardo Araújo Ribeiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing Latte — a gamified chatbot to support the production of scientific articles.

item.title: BI for mental health: an undergraduate thesis builds dashboards for the Psychosocial Care Network
item.description: Luis Bruno Fidelis Gomes defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing Business Intelligence dashboards for monitoring by professionals of Brazil’s Psychosocial Care Network.

item.title: CEDIS Signs International Agreement
item.description: The agreement between CEDIS and LabVista (Portugal) aims to promote significant advances in technological and academic research.

item.title: Before the generative AI boom, an undergraduate thesis was already teaching machines to grade essays
item.description: Back in 2016, well before the LLM frenzy, Cristóvão Frinhani defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that uses semantic similarity and machine learning to support distance-learning tutors grading essay questions.

item.title: Ontologies to help mapmakers: an undergraduate thesis tackles the hardest step of systematic mapping
item.description: Igor de Araújo Ramos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of ontologies to support the thorniest step of systematic mappings: turning collected data into a knowledge map that is actually usable.

item.title: Informational Management Support System for MCTI
item.description: Ongoing project focused on supporting informational management at the Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation.

item.title: Serviços PI
item.description: Ongoing project on innovation and efficiency in retrieving intellectual property information at INPI.
